title: NOAA NSSL CLAMPS2 TROPoe Retrievals
institutions: Ceres; National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration
topics: Atmospheric and Environmental Gas Dynamics; Environmental Monitoring and Data Management
abstract: These files contain 24-hour periods of retrieved thermodynamic profiles derived from observations collected by the CLAMPS2 Atmospheric Emitted Radiance Interferometer (AERI). retrievals were processed in IDL (e.g., AERIoe version) TROPoe algorithm (see Turner and Loehnert 2014; Blumberg 2019). data during SPLASH-SAIL project. Quicklook imagery included QL.zip. The AERI consists a Fourier transform interferometer, scene scanning-optics, IR detector, calibration blackbodies, instrument control hardware. exact system design extensive theory operation can be found Knuteson et al. (2004). On clear sky day it is capable measuring radiances throughout depth atmosphere with wavenumber resolution 1 cm<sup>-1 </sup>and temporal ~20 seconds. has an absolute accuracy < 1% ambient blackbody radiance typical noise 0.2 mW ( m<sup>2</sup>sr cm<sup>-1</sup>)<sup> -1</sup>. not able to collect precipitation when detected mechanical hatch closed protect instrument. information that used obtain temperature, water vapor trace gases as well basic cloud properties. Complete detail README file.

title: Coherent Structures in the Atmospheric Boundary Layer Measured by Dual Doppler Lidar
topics: Atmospheric aerosols and clouds; Meteorological Phenomena and Simulations; Wind and Air Flow Studies
abstract: Coherent structures describe quasi-periodic patterns in the turbulent atmospheric wind field. They have an impact fluxes and transport processes. Using Dual-Doppler Lidar techniques enables to measure horizontal field with a high temporal spatial resolution. Comparing characterization data meteorological recordings allows asses triggering mechanisms for formation of coherent surface layer.

title: Cloud base height determines fog occurrence patterns in the Namib Desert
topics: Atmospheric aerosols and clouds; Aeolian processes and effects; Meteorological Phenomena and Simulations
abstract: In the hyper-arid Namib Desert, fog serves as only regular source of moisture, vital for sustaining local ecosystems. While occurrence in region is typically associated with advection marine stratus clouds and their interaction topography, its spatial distribution strongly influenced by cloud base height, which remains poorly understood. To address this gap, study utilizes ground-based remote sensing in-situ observations to analyze systematic temporal patterns height. Our results reveal clear seasonality a diurnal cycle, lowering moderately (10–50 m h$^{−1}$) during evening early night, lifting rapidly (30–150 after sunrise, especially inland. Additionally, findings indicate that these rates are horizontal gradients thickness. Quantile regression highlights tight relationship between height near-surface relative humidity ($r$ $\approx$ -0,76) expected well-mixed boundary layer, can therefore be employed estimate across FogNet sites. case study, potential value estimated separating from low satellite-based products shown. future, full integration low-cloud product enable spatially continuous mapping first time, would facilitate ecological impact studies.

title: UAH MIPS X-Band Profiling Radar (XPR) Data. Version 1.0
topics: Meteorological Phenomena and Simulations; Soil Moisture and Remote Sensing
abstract: This data set contains radial wind and reflectivity profiles from the University of Alabama-Huntsville (UAH) vertically-pointing X-Band Profiling Radar (XPR) during 2016 VORTEX-SE field season. The XPR is part UAH Mobile Integrated System (MIPS) system. Data were collected for IOP1, 2, 3, 4B, 4C, 4D. are in both raw scan files processed universal format files.

title: Fpga Based Implementation Of Simplified Space Vector Pwm Algorithm For Multilevel Inverter Fed Induction Motor Drives
topics: Multilevel Inverters and Converters; Power Systems and Renewable Energy; Induction Heating and Inverter Technology
abstract: Space Vector Pulse Width Modulation is popular for variable frequency drives. The method has several advantages over carried based PWM and computation intensive. implementation of SVPWM multilevel inverter requires special attention at the same time consumes considerable resources. Due to faster processing power reduced all computational burden, FPGAs are being investigated as an alternative other controllers. In this paper, a space vector algorithm implemented using FPGA which less area modular in structure. verified experimentally Neutral Point Clamped development board xc3s5000-4fg900.

title: NCAR Global Climate Four-Dimensional Data Assimilation (CFDDA) Hourly 40 km Reanalysis
topics: Meteorological Phenomena and Simulations; Climate variability and models; Atmospheric and Environmental Gas Dynamics
abstract: NCAR Global Climate Four-Dimensional Data Assimilation (CFDDA) Hourly 40 km Reanalysis dataset is a dynamically-downscaled with high temporal and spatial resolution that was created using NCAR's CFDDA system. At the core of system 5th generation Pennsylvania State University / mesoscale model (MM5).The contains three-dimensional hourly analyses in netCDF format for global atmospheric state from 1985 to 2005 on horizontal grid (0.4 degree increment) 28 vertical levels. As result, provides collection hourly, meso-beta scale reanalyses files all days months within 21-year period, providing good representation local forcing diurnal variation processes planetary boundary layer. time its creation, this only reanalysis full fields available at intervals.The generated by continuously assimilating standard surface upper-air observations. 6-hourly data measurements (primarily 12- 24-hourly rawinsondes) were assimilated. Initial land conditions based NASA Land System (GLDAS) 1 latitude-longitude grid, compiled Noah (LSM), where GLDAS values substrate soil moisture temperature, ground skin snow water equivalent used. Daily sea temperatures (SSTs) specified National Centers Environmental Prediction (NCEP) Version 2.0 daily SST defined 0.25 grid.

title: TEAMx 2025 Eddy Covariance Station Data, Nafingalm
institutions: Deutsches Zentrum für Luft- und Raumfahrt e. V. (DLR)
abstract: This dataset contains sonic anemometer (Metek uSonic Class A), T/RH probe, and solar radiation (CNR4) data from an eddy-covariance (EC) station placed at Nafingalm, Austria during the 2025 TEAMx campaign. The EC is located 47.2120094 N,11.7131157 E 1929 m above sea level. Data organized in daily files. was installed 5 AGL facing 255°, with primary flow along North-South plane. Wind measurements have been rotated into meteorological coordinate system. 4-component radiometer temperature/humidity sensors were 2 AGL. Radiation manufacturer-derived calibrations applied. Additional input data in cloud base height collocated ceilometer GML temperature, water vapor mixing ratio, pressure sensor attached to MWR housing. title: Spatial and temporal variability of soil water content in two regions of Southwest German
topics: Soil Moisture and Remote Sensing; Hydrology and Watershed Management Studies; Plant Water Relations and Carbon Dynamics
abstract: Soil water content (SWC) plays a key role in partitioning of energy and fluxes on the land surface. Knowledge about spatial temporal variability topsoil is crucial for understanding surface processes, improving climate hydrology modeling. In recent study, we investigated SWC variability, its relation to mean soil (qm) rainfall regional event scales. To accomplish this, used multi-year high resolution data set, obtained from two moisture sensor networks (spatial extent 25 km ×25 km), set up at croplands Kraichgau Swabian Alb regions Southwest Germany. abstract: Virtual tower data consist of profiles wind speed and direction at a number locations near the A1 site. title: Atmospheric Surface Flux Station #30 measurements (level 2 Processed), Study of Precipitation, the Lower Atmosphere and Surface for Hydrometeorology (SPLASH), September 2021-July 2023
institutions: NOAA Physical Sciences Laboratory; Cooperative Institute for Research in Environmental Sciences; NOAA Air Resources Laboratory
topics: Meteorological Phenomena and Simulations; Atmospheric aerosols and clouds; Precipitation Measurement and Analysis
abstract: Processed (Level 2) measurements and derived variables from the Atmospheric Surface Flux Station #30 (ASFS-30) deployed at Kettle Ponds Annex site (38°56.3686' N, 106°58.1781' W) during Study of Precipitation, Lower Atmosphere, for Hydrometeorology (SPLASH) campaign near Gothic, Colorado, September 2021 through July 2023. The ASFS measured surface energy budget, momentum flux, near-surface meteorology, soil properties. Measurements high-resolution 3-dimensional winds were observed a nominal height 4.6 m (depending on snow depth). upwelling broadband radiation meteorology 2.9 m. are included in three netCDF files per day. "sledmet" comprised 1-min averages variables, including skin temperature, depth, radiative fluxes, conductive fluxes. "sledseb" 10-min same as also include calculations turbulent sensible latent heat associated diagnostics, stress, Monin-Obukhov parameters using both eddy covariance bulk methodologies, all valid intervals. Both these file types contain "_qc" variable paired with each measurement variable, or family that is temporally-matched quality control code: 0 = good data, 1 caution (data may be suspect), 2 bad -1 missing (no data was collected). "10hz" gas densities water vapor carbon dioxide quality-controlled, aggregated to regular 10-Hz temporal grid, (for winds) rotated into earth coordinate frame. A detailed documentation conditions, processing steps taken construct this set, other caveats uncertainties will provided an accompanying published manuscript. Note update: v2_1 update provides double rotation ("dbl") fluxes addition planar fit ("pf"), well corrects problem v2 primarily affects only includes updates sledseb set: sledmet sledwind10hz, continue refer set.

title: Atmospheric Surface Flux Station #30 measurements (level 1 Raw), Study of Precipitation, the Lower Atmosphere and Surface for Hydrometeorology (SPLASH), September 2021-July 2023
institutions: NOAA Physical Sciences Laboratory; Cooperative Institute for Research in Environmental Sciences; Rocky Mountain Biological Laboratory
topics: Meteorological Phenomena and Simulations; Atmospheric aerosols and clouds; Precipitation Measurement and Analysis
abstract: Raw (Level 1) measurements from the Atmospheric Surface Flux Station #30 (ASFS-30) deployed at Kettle Ponds Annex site (38°56.3686' N, 106°58.1781' W) during Study of Precipitation, Lower Atmosphere, and for Hydrometeorology (SPLASH) campaign near Gothic, Colorado, September 2021 through July 2023. The ASFS measured variables comprising surface energy budget, momentum flux, near-surface meteorology, soil properties. These are included in three netCDF files per day. "slow" 1-minute averages variables, including height change (due to accumulating/ablating snow), upwelling downwelling shortwave longwave radiative fluxes. "fast" data 20 Hz resolution 3-dimensional wind, temperature, gas concentrations water vapor carbon dioxide. raw with technical corrections applied but no quality assurance. A detailed documentation measurement will be provided a forthcoming publication. For scientific purposes, we recommend using Level 2 when they available, as these include full control well higher-order derived products.

title: NOAA PSL CL31 Ceilometer Backscatter and Cloud Base Height Data for SPLASH
institutions: Cooperative Institute for Research in Environmental Sciences; University of Colorado Boulder; NOAA Physical Sciences Laboratory
topics: Calibration and Measurement Techniques; Atmospheric Ozone and Climate; Atmospheric and Environmental Gas Dynamics
abstract: This dataset contains daily files from a CL31 ceilometer manufactured by Vaisala that was deployed at Roaring Judy in the East River Watershed Colorado (38.7169321 N, 106.853031 W, 2494 m above mean sea level) 21 October 2021 to 28 January 2022 as part of National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) Study Precipitation, Lower Atmosphere, Surface for Hydrometeorology (SPLASH) campaign. The contain backscatter profiles, cloud base heights, visibility. measures vertical profiles using laser technology. From height visibility are determined with software CL-view. For details on instrument specifics methods, see manufacturer manual (cl31usersguide.pdf). file format is original (.DAT). description manual. To convert .DAT netcdf format, open source command line Python program ‘cl2nc’ (https://github.com/peterkuma/cl2nc) can, example, be used. naming conventions follows: NOAA_PSL_CL31_Roaring Judy_yyyymmdd_HH.DAT yyyy: Year mm: Month dd: Day HH: Hour when first sample written time stamp all data UTC.

title: University of Nebraska-Lincoln Unmanned Aerial System observations from LAPSE-RATE
topics: Astronomical Observations and Instrumentation; Gamma-ray bursts and supernovae; Scientific Research and Discoveries
abstract: This dataset contains thermodynamic (pressure, temperature, humidity) measurements from the University of Nebraska-Lincoln unmanned aerial system multirotors during LAPSE-RATE campaign 14--19 July 2018. 171 files two multirotors. File names are coded as per community standard naming agreement. See README_v1.txt for more information about convention and other details.
